evals.name: brainstorm-fuzzy-requirement
evals.prompt: I want users to be able to share their trips somehow and maybe edit them together? Not totally sure what that should look like.
expected_output: Agent read preconditions (constitution, reference-index, relevant code, history) before shaping
Mirrored the PROBLEM back (no in-app co-planning today) rather than jumping to a solution
Asked clarifying questions one topic at a time (real-time vs async, roles, conflict handling)
Offered concrete shapes grounded in existing integrations (reuse django-guardian) instead of inventing dependencies
Wrote NOTHING to disk — no PRD.md, no user-stories.md, no scratch file
Converged on a landed requirement and offered to continue into define in the same conversation

evals.name: define-new-spec
evals.prompt: Ok the requirement is clear: invitation-based async collaboration on itineraries, roles owner/editor/reader, last-write-wins, reusing django-guardian. Let's write the spec.
expected_output: .ai/specs/<slug>/PRD.md created from prd-template.md, one-to-two screens, no implementation detail
A short uppercase story prefix was proposed and confirmed, recorded in PRD frontmatter (prefix:)
.ai/specs/<slug>/user-stories.md created with <PREFIX>-NNN IDs, each a deliverable increment
Each story has Given/When/Then acceptance criteria split into Happy path and Sad paths
The created_by-vs-owner mismatch was surfaced and resolved via a question, not guessed
PRD Open questions section is empty; no [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] blocks anywhere
Agent paused for explicit approval; no source code written; status stays draft until approved

evals.name: define-amend-corrective-story
evals.prompt: While building ITIN-005 we found sessions expire mid-edit and silently log people out — we need to fix it for the spec to hold. Add it to the spec.

context.existing_stories: ITIN-001 through ITIN-007, highest ID is ITIN-007

expected_output: Agent recognized this is an amendment to an active spec, not a new spec
Read existing user-stories.md first; did NOT renumber existing IDs
Appended a new corrective story continuing the count (ITIN-008)
Story is framed as corrective and references the story it surfaced during
Story has Happy and Sad path Given/When/Then criteria
Reused the spec's existing prefix; did not invent a new one
Did not write source code (the fix itself is planning's job)

evals.name: archive-completed-spec
evals.prompt: All the collaboration stories are done. Let's archive this spec.
expected_output: Verified every story is checked before proceeding
Drafted .ai/specs/<slug>/archive.md from spec-archive-template.md, concise, IN ENGLISH
Archive lists stories delivered and the plan summaries that built them
Proposed ADRs / brain updates (or explicitly stated none) WITHOUT writing to adr-log.md
Asked for explicit confirmation before the destructive step
On confirmation, used scripts/archive.sh: moved doc to .ai/memory/archive/specs/<date>-<slug>.md and removed the spec folder
Handed off to update-brain for any proposed ADRs

evals.name: archive-blocked-open-stories
evals.prompt: Let's close and archive the collaboration spec.
expected_output: Agent detected at least one open story and STOPPED — did not archive
Surfaced the specific open story (ITIN-009)
Offered the three options: finish it via planning, drop it (record in Deviations), or split into a follow-up spec
Did not run archive.sh and did not delete the spec folder
